channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Leveraging Computerized Adaptive Testing for Cost-effective Evaluation of Large Language Models in Medical Benchmarking
item.description: arXiv:2603.23506v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid proliferation of large language models (LLMs) in healthcare creates an urgent need for scalable and psychometrically sound evaluation methods. Conventional static benchmarks are costly to administer repeatedly, vulnerable to data contaminati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, LLM, framework, tool, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Fast and Faithful: Real-Time Verification for Long-Document Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.23508v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is increasingly deployed in enterprise search and document-centric assistants, where responses must be grounded in long and complex source materials. In practice, verifying that generated answers faithfully reflect...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, product, LLM, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Internal Safety Collapse in Frontier Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.23509v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This work identifies a critical failure mode in frontier large language models (LLMs), which we term Internal Safety Collapse (ISC): under certain task conditions, models enter a state in which they continuously generate harmful content while executin...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, framework, tool, model, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: S-Path-RAG: Semantic-Aware Shortest-Path Retrieval Augmented Generation for Multi-Hop Knowledge Graph Question Answering
item.description: arXiv:2603.23512v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present S-Path-RAG, a semantic-aware shortest-path Retrieval-Augmented Generation framework designed to improve multi-hop question answering over large knowledge graphs. S-Path-RAG departs from one-shot, text-heavy retrieval by enumerating bounded-...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, framework, arxiv, model, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Berta: an open-source, modular tool for AI-enabled clinical documentation
item.description: arXiv:2603.23513v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Commercial AI scribes cost \$99-600 per physician per month, operate as opaque systems, and do not return data to institutional infrastructure, limiting organizational control over data governance, quality improvement, and clinical workflows. We devel...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> audio, platform, tool, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DepthCharge: A Domain-Agnostic Framework for Measuring Depth-Dependent Knowledge in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.23514v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models appear competent when answering general questions but often fail when pushed into domain-specific details. No existing methodology provides an out-of-the-box solution for measuring how deeply LLMs can sustain accurate responses u...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, LLM, framework, analysis, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Training a Large Language Model for Medical Coding Using Privacy-Preserving Synthetic Clinical Data
item.description: arXiv:2603.23515v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Improving the accuracy and reliability of medical coding reduces clinician burnout and supports revenue cycle processes, freeing providers to focus more on patient care. However, automating the assignment of ICD-10-CM and CPT codes from clinical docum...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, fine-tuning, zero-shot, template, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PLDR-LLMs Reason At Self-Organized Criticality
item.description: arXiv:2603.23539v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We show that PLDR-LLMs pretrained at self-organized criticality exhibit reasoning at inference time. The characteristics of PLDR-LLM deductive outputs at criticality is similar to second-order phase transitions. At criticality, the correlation length ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, LLM, model, large language model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Environment Maps: Structured Environmental Representations for Long-Horizon Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.23610v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although large language models (LLMs) have advanced rapidly, robust automation of complex software workflows remains an open problem. In long-horizon settings, agents frequently suffer from cascading errors and environmental stochasticity; a single mi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, paper, LLM, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Evaluating a Multi-Agent Voice-Enabled Smart Speaker for Care Homes: A Safety-Focused Framework
item.description: arXiv:2603.23625v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly being explored in health and social care to reduce administrative workload and allow staff to spend more time on patient care. This paper evaluates a voice-enabled Care Home Smart Speaker designed to suppor...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, paper, framework, arxiv, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Can LLM Agents Be CFOs? A Benchmark for Resource Allocation in Dynamic Enterprise Environments
item.description: arXiv:2603.23638v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have enabled agentic systems that can reason, plan, and act across complex tasks, but it remains unclear whether they can allocate resources effectively under uncertainty. Unlike short-horizon reactive decisions, allocatio...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, LLM, tool, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GTO Wizard Benchmark
item.description: arXiv:2603.23660v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce GTO Wizard Benchmark, a public API and standardized evaluation framework for benchmarking algorithms in Heads-Up No-Limit Texas Hold'em (HUNL). The benchmark evaluates agents against GTO Wizard AI, a state-of-the-art superhuman poker agen...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, reasoning, ICL, LLM, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LLMs Do Not Grade Essays Like Humans
item.description: arXiv:2603.23714v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models have recently been proposed as tools for automated essay scoring, but their agreement with human grading remains unclear. In this work, we evaluate how LLM-generated scores compare with human grades and analyze the grading behavi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, tool, model, GPT, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learning-guided Prioritized Planning for Lifelong Multi-Agent Path Finding in Warehouse Automation
item.description: arXiv:2603.23838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Lifelong Multi-Agent Path Finding (MAPF) is critical for modern warehouse automation, which requires multiple robots to continuously navigate conflict-free paths to optimize the overall system throughput. However, the complexity of warehouse environme...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, framework, arxiv, RAG, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: VehicleMemBench: An Executable Benchmark for Multi-User Long-Term Memory in In-Vehicle Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.23840v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With the growing demand for intelligent in-vehicle experiences, vehicle-based agents are evolving from simple assistants to long-term companions. This evolution requires agents to continuously model multi-user preferences and make reliable decisions i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, ICL, LLM, release, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Implicit Turn-Wise Policy Optimization for Proactive User-LLM Interaction
item.description: arXiv:2603.23550v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-turn human-AI collaboration is fundamental to deploying interactive services such as adaptive tutoring, conversational recommendation, and professional consultation. However, optimizing these interactions via reinforcement learning is hindered b...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, LLM, analysis, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Safe Reinforcement Learning with Preference-based Constraint Inference
item.description: arXiv:2603.23565v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Safe reinforcement learning (RL) is a standard paradigm for safety-critical decision making. However, real-world safety constraints can be complex, subjective, and even hard to explicitly specify. Existing works on constraint inference rely on restric...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> demonstration, study, model, arxiv, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: StateLinFormer: Stateful Training Enhancing Long-term Memory in Navigation
item.description: arXiv:2603.23571v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Effective navigation intelligence relies on long-term memory to support both immediate generalization and sustained adaptation. However, existing approaches face a dilemma: modular systems rely on explicit mapping but lack flexibility, while Transform...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, ICL, transformer, memory, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt engineering, context window, context, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, augmented, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, LLM, framework, vector, knowledge base | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Causal Reconstruction of Sentiment Signals from Sparse News Data
item.description: arXiv:2603.23568v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sentiment signals derived from sparse news are commonly used in financial analysis and technology monitoring, yet transforming raw article-level observations into reliable temporal series remains a largely unsolved engineering problem. Rather than tre...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, framework, analysis, arxiv, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> chain-of-thought, reasoning, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Beyond Masks: Efficient, Flexible Diffusion Language Models via Deletion-Insertion Processes
item.description: arXiv:2603.23507v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While Masked Diffusion Language Models (MDLMs) relying on token masking and unmasking have shown promise in language modeling, their computational efficiency and generation flexibility remain constrained by the masking paradigm. In this paper, we prop...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, paper, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Visuospatial Perspective Taking in Multimodal Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.23510v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As multimodal language models (MLMs) are increasingly used in social and collaborative settings, it is crucial to evaluate their perspective-taking abilities. Existing benchmarks largely rely on text-based vignettes or static scene understanding, leav...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, context, model, multimodal | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Grounding Vision and Language to 3D Masks for Long-Horizon Box Rearrangement
item.description: arXiv:2603.23676v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study long-horizon planning in 3D environments from under-specified natural-language goals using only visual observations, focusing on multi-step 3D box rearrangement tasks. Existing approaches typically rely on symbolic planners with brittle relat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, reasoning, vision, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Upper Entropy for 2-Monotone Lower Probabilities
item.description: arXiv:2603.23558v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Uncertainty quantification is a key aspect in many tasks such as model selection/regularization, or quantifying prediction uncertainties to perform active learning or OOD detection. Within credal approaches that consider modeling uncertainty as probab...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, arxiv, analysis, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Synthetic Mixed Training: Scaling Parametric Knowledge Acquisition Beyond RAG
item.description: arXiv:2603.23562v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Synthetic data augmentation helps language models learn new knowledge in data-constrained domains. However, naively scaling existing synthetic data methods by training on more synthetic tokens or using stronger generators yields diminishing returns be...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, RAG, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AscendOptimizer: Episodic Agent for Ascend NPU Operator Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2603.23566v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AscendC (Ascend C) operator optimization on Huawei Ascend neural processing units (NPUs) faces a two-fold knowledge bottleneck: unlike the CUDA ecosystem, there are few public reference implementations to learn from, and performance hinges on a couple...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> instruction, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt engineering, example, prompt, tool, step-by-step | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: DISCO: Document Intelligence Suite for COmparative Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2603.23511v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Document intelligence requires accurate text extraction and reliable reasoning over document content. We introduce \textbf{DISCO}, a \emph{Document Intelligence Suite for COmparative Evaluation}, that evaluates optical character recognition (OCR) pipe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, prompting, vision, prompt, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Efficient Benchmarking of AI Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.23749v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating AI agents on comprehensive benchmarks is expensive because each evaluation requires interactive rollouts with tool use and multi-step reasoning. We study whether small task subsets can preserve agent rankings at substantially lower cost. Un...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, framework, tool, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond Accuracy: Introducing a Symbolic-Mechanistic Approach to Interpretable Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2603.23517v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accuracy-based evaluation cannot reliably distinguish genuine generalization from shortcuts like memorization, leakage, or brittle heuristics, especially in small-data regimes. In this position paper, we argue for mechanism-aware evaluation that combi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PoiCGAN: A Targeted Poisoning Based on Feature-Label Joint Perturbation in Federated Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.23574v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Federated Learning (FL), as a popular distributed learning paradigm, has shown outstanding performance in improving computational efficiency and protecting data privacy, and is widely applied in industrial image classification. However, due to its dis...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, arxiv, model, image | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
